host: darwin-arm64 (Mac local — read-only investigation, no GPU fire)
goal: Close §11 PTX-emit cleanup pass — eliminate redundant non-.global wmma.{load,store,mma} stub variants in step1-equivalent emit.
verdict: NO-OP — premise not reproducible; no redundant emit found in current codegen
honest_scope_g3: Investigation only. Source files unchanged. F-RFC067-PASSTHROUGH-PRESERVED + F-RFC055-CPU-CODEGEN-UNTOUCHED trivially hold (zero diff).

evidence.lower_stmt_emit_count_per_op: Each gpu_wmma_load_a / _b / _mma / _store_c emits EXACTLY ONE PTX instruction via out.push(_nvptx_instr(wmma_mnem, ...)). No double-emit, no fall-through second branch.

grep_global_variant.interpretation: .global-suffixed wmma mnemonics DO NOT appear anywhere in the codegen source tree.

grep_all_wmma_emit_sites_in_codegen.interpretation: 13 emit-string literals total — 4 from the canonical PR #150 table (f16×f16→f32 .shared family) + 9 from the P3 family-keyed table (bf16/f16-acc/f16). Each is the single mnemonic returned for one (op, dtype, layout) tuple. None of the 13 is a non-.global redundant scaffold of another.

hand_emit_global_ptx_location.interpretation: These .global mnemonics live in a HAND-EMITTED PTX fixture used for ptxas-accept smokes (Round 8 §3b f16×f16→f16 family), NOT in the codegen emit path. Hand-emit fixtures exercise the ptxas frontend directly; the codegen never produces these strings.

parse_gate.result: OK: parses cleanly (baseline only — no edit to revalidate)

lower_test_status: Not invoked (no source change → no regression to verify). Case 23 (`_test_wmma_instr_emit`) assertions at nvptx_lower_test.hexa:2434-2441 already pin the canonical .shared mnemonics, so any future non-.global redundant emit would already be caught by these substring checks (no scaffold-stub leak guard at L2453-2458 also covers this).

task_premise_review.task_claims: Step1 PTX emits BOTH `wmma.load.a.sync.aligned.row.m16n16k16.global.f16` (canonical, used) AND `wmma.load.a.sync.aligned.row.m16n16k16.f16` (no .global, redundant scaffold-stub).
task_premise_review.code_truth: Codegen emits ONE variant per op-key: `wmma.load.a.sync.aligned.row.m16n16k16.f16.shared` (note `.shared`, not `.global`). The .shared form is the RFC 067 P2 design — load/store fragments via a 2048 B `_hexa_wmma_stage` shared-memory slot (compiler/codegen/nvptx_target.hexa:620-623). Going through `.global` directly would skip the staging buffer entirely.
task_premise_review.possible_origin: The `.global` variants in the task description match the hand-emit PTX fixture in rfc067_pA_round8 (lines 11-14 of hand_emit_ptx.txt), which is a SEPARATE ptxas-accept smoke for the f16×f16→f16 family — not output by codegen. The task may have conflated the two paths.
task_premise_review.inbox/fires/rfc067_p6_revalidate_2026_05: Referenced in task description but does not exist in tree. Cannot cross-check the claimed §11 cleanup item against its source.

recommendation.action: Reject the cleanup as not-applicable. The codegen already emits one mnemonic per op-key (no redundancy to remove).
recommendation.if_user_wants_global_path: That would be a NEW emit path (skip .shared staging, load fragments directly from device DRAM). Separate design decision, not a 'cleanup'. Would touch _nvptx_wmma_mnemonic + _nvptx_wmma_mnemonic_family + STMT_CALL lowering branch + Case 23 + per-op offset logic — non-trivial; not a cosmetic edit.
recommendation.if_user_meant_drop_shared_suffix: The `.shared` state-space suffix is REQUIRED by PTX ISA §9.7.13.4 for wmma.load/store when the address operand points at a `.shared` slot (which is what `_hexa_wmma_stage` is — see line 812 P2 declaration). Removing `.shared` would make ptxas reject the instruction. NOT a safe cleanup.

f_cpu_codegen_untouched: TRIVIAL PASS (zero edits to x86_64_linux / arm64_darwin / thumbv7em_eabihf)
